title: 5V Reference Circuit Test
instruction: With key ON engine OFF use a DMM set to DC volts. Backprobe the TPS signal wire at the PCM connector. Measure voltage between signal and signal ground.
expected_result: Should read 0.5-0.9V at closed throttle or near 5V on reference pin. Reading near 0V or 5V on signal with throttle closed indicates circuit problem.

title: 5V Reference Circuit Test
instruction: Measure voltage on the 5V reference wire going to TPS at the sensor connector with key ON engine OFF. Compare to APP1 and MAP sensor reference pins.
expected_result: All three sensors on 5V_REF_A circuit should show same reference voltage. If TPS and APP1 and MAP all read low (under 4.5V) the PCM 5V reference output is faulty.

title: 5V Reference Circuit Test
instruction: Disconnect each sensor on the 5V_REF_A circuit one at a time while monitoring the reference voltage. If voltage returns to 5V after disconnecting a sensor that sensor has an internal short pulling the reference low.
expected_result: Voltage should return to 4.9-5.1V when the shorted sensor is disconnected. If voltage remains low with all sensors disconnected the PCM reference circuit is failed.

title: 5V Reference Circuit Test
instruction: Inspect wiring harness between PCM and all sensors on the 5V_REF_A circuit for chafing pinched wires moisture intrusion or corrosion at connector pins.
expected_result: No damage to wiring or connectors. Clean dry connector pins with no green or white corrosion deposits.

title: TAC Module Communication Test
instruction: Connect a scan tool capable of reading enhanced data. Navigate to TAC module data. Check for TAC module present and responding on CAN bus.
expected_result: TAC module should show as communicating with PCM. Communication fault will show as no response or timeout on CAN_HS network.

title: TAC Module Communication Test
instruction: With scan tool monitor TAC commanded throttle position versus actual throttle position in real time while slowly pressing accelerator pedal.
expected_result: Commanded and actual throttle position should track within 2% of each other. A deviation greater than 5% indicates TAC mechanical or control fault.

title: TAC Module Communication Test
instruction: Inspect TAC module CAN bus wiring. Check CAN_H and CAN_L terminals at TAC module connector for correct resistance. With all modules connected CAN bus should read 60 ohms between CAN_H and CAN_L.
expected_result: Should read approximately 60 ohms indicating two 120-ohm termination resistors in parallel. Reading over 120 ohms indicates an open circuit. Reading near 0 ohms indicates a short.

title: TAC Module Communication Test
instruction: Perform a TAC module relearn procedure using scan tool if CAN communication is restored. Clear codes and verify reduced engine power condition clears.
expected_result: Engine should return to full power operation. Throttle should respond normally to pedal input. No TAC-related codes should return.

title: PCM Power and Ground Test
instruction: Locate all PCM power supply fuses and relays. Check fuse condition with DMM for voltage on both sides of each fuse with key ON.
expected_result: All PCM power fuses should show battery voltage on both sides when key is ON. An open fuse indicates PCM power supply fault.

title: PCM Power and Ground Test
instruction: With key ON backprobe all PCM ground pins at PCM connector. Measure resistance from each ground pin to chassis ground with KOEO (key on engine off).
expected_result: Each ground circuit should measure under 0.1 ohm to chassis. Any ground reading over 0.5 ohm indicates a poor ground connection that can cause PCM processor faults.

title: PCM Power and Ground Test
instruction: Start engine if possible. Monitor PCM supply voltage using scan tool parameter. Voltage should be stable between 12.5V and 14.5V at idle. Rapid fluctuation indicates charging system issue affecting PCM.
expected_result: PCM supply voltage should be stable. Fluctuation of more than 0.5V indicates alternator noise or poor ground connection which can cause PCM processor faults.

title: PCM Power and Ground Test
instruction: If all power and ground circuits test good and PCM P0606 persists contact PCM manufacturer for reflash or replacement. Document all DTCs and freeze frame data before replacing PCM.
expected_result: After reflash or replacement all previous PCM faults should be resolved. Perform complete system relearn including idle relearn and throttle position relearn.

title: MAF Sensor Circuit Test
instruction: With engine running at warm idle observe MAF reading on scan tool. At idle a healthy MAF should read between 2 and 8 g/s depending on engine size. A zero reading with engine running indicates circuit or sensor fault.
expected_result: MAF should show a positive reading at idle. Zero MAF with engine running indicates open circuit failed sensor or contaminated sensor element.

title: MAF Sensor Circuit Test
instruction: With key ON engine OFF use DMM to verify 12V supply and ground at MAF sensor connector. Verify 5V reference if applicable to sensor type.
expected_result: Should show battery voltage on power pin and less than 0.1V on ground pin. Absent voltage indicates wiring fault before sensor.

title: MAF Sensor Circuit Test
instruction: Start engine. Lightly tap MAF sensor body with finger while watching MAF reading on scan tool. An intermittent dropout or spike during tapping indicates a failing sensor element.
expected_result: MAF reading should remain stable during tapping. Any dropout or spike during light tapping indicates sensor element failure requiring replacement.

title: MAF Sensor Circuit Test
instruction: Remove MAF sensor and inspect sensor wire element for contamination or damage. Inspect air filter housing for evidence of oil contamination. Clean sensor with MAF cleaner if contaminated.
expected_result: Sensor wire element should be clean and intact. Oil contamination from overboosted PCV or soaked air filter is a common cause of MAF failure.

title: APP Sensor Correlation Test
instruction: Using a scan tool monitor APP sensor D (APP1) and APP sensor E (APP2) simultaneously. At rest both should read near 0 percent. APP1 and APP2 should track each other with APP1 reading approximately twice APP2 through the full pedal range.
expected_result: At idle APP1 should read 0-5% and APP2 should read 0-2.5%. Through full pedal sweep the ratio should remain near 2:1. Any deviation over 5% indicates sensor fault.

title: APP Sensor Correlation Test
instruction: Check APP sensor harness for damaged or corroded pins at the accelerator pedal module connector. Both sensors share a common connector and any corrosion can affect one or both signals.
expected_result: Connector pins should be clean dry and properly seated. No bent or pushed-back pins. No green corrosion on signal or reference pins.

title: APP Sensor Correlation Test
instruction: Measure reference voltage supply to both APP sensors with key ON. APP1 uses 5V_REF_A and APP2 uses 5V_REF_B. Both should show 4.9-5.1V at reference pins.
expected_result: Both reference voltages should be within 0.1V of 5.0V. A low reference voltage will cause sensor reading to be proportionally low indicating PCM 5V reference circuit fault not sensor fault.

title: APP Sensor Correlation Test
instruction: If reference voltages are correct and APP sensors still show correlation fault replace the accelerator pedal position sensor module. This is a non-serviceable assembly containing both sensors.
expected_result: After replacement perform accelerator pedal position sensor relearn procedure per manufacturer service information. Verify APP1 and APP2 track correctly through full pedal sweep.

title: Fuel Trim Diagnosis
instruction: With engine at operating temperature (ECT over 180°F) monitor short term fuel trim and long term fuel trim at idle. Combined STFT plus LTFT over 25% indicates significant lean condition.
expected_result: At warm idle combined fuel trim should be within -10% to +10%. Values consistently over +20% indicate a lean condition requiring further investigation.

title: Fuel Trim Diagnosis
instruction: With engine idling spray carburetor cleaner in short bursts around intake manifold gaskets throttle body base gasket and vacuum hose connections. Watch STFT on scan tool for drops indicating a vacuum leak found.
expected_result: STFT should drop 5-10% or RPM should increase momentarily when cleaner hits a vacuum leak. No response indicates no vacuum leak at that location. Be careful near ignition sources.

title: Fuel Trim Diagnosis
instruction: Check fuel pressure with engine running. Low fuel pressure (more than 10% below specification) combined with high positive fuel trim indicates fuel delivery concern. Check fuel pump pressure and volume.
expected_result: Fuel pressure should meet manufacturer specification typically 55-60 PSI for returnless systems. Low pressure indicates weak fuel pump failing fuel pressure regulator or restricted fuel filter.

title: Fuel Trim Diagnosis
instruction: Perform injector balance test with scan tool to verify all injectors are flowing equal amounts. One low-flow injector can cause lean condition on that cylinder affecting overall fuel trim.
expected_result: All injectors should show similar contribution values within 5% of each other. A low-contribution injector indicates clogged or failed injector.

title: O2 Sensor Evaluation
instruction: With engine fully warm monitor upstream O2 sensor voltage on scan tool. A healthy sensor should switch rapidly between 0.1V and 0.9V approximately 1-4 times per second at warm idle.
expected_result: Upstream O2 sensor should be actively switching. A sensor stuck high (rich) stuck low (lean) or switching slowly (under 0.5 Hz) indicates a lazy or failed sensor.

title: O2 Sensor Evaluation
instruction: Monitor downstream O2 sensor voltage. The downstream sensor after catalytic converter should show a stable voltage around 0.6-0.7V on a good catalyst. An active switching downstream sensor indicates catalyst efficiency fault P0420.
expected_result: Downstream sensor should be relatively stable. Compare upstream and downstream switching rates. Downstream switching at similar rate to upstream confirms catalyst inefficiency.

title: O2 Sensor Evaluation
instruction: Check O2 sensor heater function. Most O2 sensors have internal heaters that bring sensor to operating temperature quickly. With engine cold start and scan tool monitor O2 sensor switching. Should begin switching within 60-90 seconds of cold start.
expected_result: O2 sensor should begin switching within 90 seconds of cold start. Slow switching after 3 minutes indicates failed heater circuit.

title: O2 Sensor Evaluation
instruction: Inspect O2 sensor for external contamination. Remove sensor and inspect tip for white silica deposits (coolant leak) black sooty deposits (oil burning) or smooth grey glaze (RTV silicone contamination). Any deposits indicate sensor requires replacement.
expected_result: O2 sensor tip should show normal light tan or grey color from combustion. Heavy deposits indicate underlying engine condition requiring investigation before replacing sensor.

title: MAP Sensor Evaluation
instruction: With key ON engine OFF MAP sensor should read approximately barometric pressure which is 95-105 kPa at sea level. With engine running at warm idle MAP should drop to 25-45 kPa indicating normal engine vacuum.
expected_result: KOEO MAP should match local barometric pressure. With engine running MAP should show significant vacuum drop. No vacuum drop with engine running indicates intake leak or MAP sensor fault.

title: MAP Sensor Evaluation
instruction: Apply vacuum to MAP sensor using a hand vacuum pump while monitoring MAP sensor output on scan tool. Apply 10 inches Hg vacuum. MAP reading should drop proportionally. At 10 in Hg MAP should read approximately 61 kPa.
expected_result: MAP reading should decrease approximately 3.4 kPa per inch of mercury applied. Linear response indicates sensor is functional. No response or incorrect response indicates sensor failure.
tool_required: Scan tool and hand vacuum pump

title: MAP Sensor Evaluation
instruction: Check MAP sensor 5V reference and ground at sensor connector. MAP uses 5V_REF_A reference. Low reference voltage will cause MAP to read proportionally low making it appear as a vacuum even when no vacuum is present.
expected_result: 5V reference pin should read 4.9-5.1V with key ON. Ground pin should read under 0.1V. Incorrect readings indicate PCM reference circuit fault not MAP sensor fault.

title: MAP Sensor Evaluation
instruction: Inspect MAP sensor vacuum hose for cracks kinks or disconnection. A cracked or disconnected MAP vacuum hose will cause MAP to read barometric pressure at all times (no vacuum signal) leading to excessive fuel delivery and rich condition.
expected_result: Vacuum hose should be intact flexible and fully seated on both MAP sensor port and intake manifold fitting. Any crack allows atmospheric air to dilute the vacuum signal.

title: Crank Sensor Test
instruction: Connect scan tool and attempt to start vehicle. Monitor RPM on scan tool during cranking. If RPM stays at zero during cranking the PCM is not receiving a crank signal. Normal crank signal shows 100-250 RPM during starter operation.
expected_result: RPM should show 100-250 RPM during cranking on scan tool. Zero RPM during cranking with engine turning confirms absent crank sensor signal.

title: Crank Sensor Test
instruction: Locate crankshaft position sensor. Check sensor air gap. For magnetic type sensors gap should be 0.030-0.060 inches. For Hall effect sensors verify trigger wheel teeth are not missing or damaged.
expected_result: Air gap should be within specification. Inspect trigger wheel for missing bent or damaged teeth. Any missing teeth will cause RPM signal dropout.

title: Crank Sensor Test
instruction: Using a DMM check crank sensor resistance (magnetic type only). Disconnect sensor and measure resistance across signal terminals. Should read 800-1200 ohms for most magnetic sensors. Open or short indicates failed sensor.
expected_result: Resistance should be within 20% of specification value. An open reading (OL) indicates broken sensor coil. Near zero ohms indicates internal short.

title: Crank Sensor Test
instruction: Check crank sensor wiring for damaged insulation chafed wires or loose connector. Crank sensor wiring passes near exhaust and moving components and is vulnerable to heat damage. Wiggle test wiring during cranking attempt.
expected_result: Wiring and connector should be intact with no damage. No change during wiggle test. If RPM signal appears intermittently during wiggle test wiring harness has intermittent open circuit.

title: Coolant Temperature Investigation
instruction: With engine cold use scan tool to verify ECT sensor reads ambient temperature within 5°C. At cold start ECT should match IAT sensor reading. A significant difference indicates possible sensor fault or cooling system issue.
expected_result: ECT and IAT should read within 5°C of each other on a cold soak (engine off for 4+ hours). Large difference indicates ECT sensor inaccuracy or engine not fully cooled.

title: Coolant Temperature Investigation
instruction: Start engine and monitor ECT rise to operating temperature. Normal warm-up time to reach 195°F is 3-7 minutes in moderate ambient temperature. Monitor for overheating above 220°F or thermostat not opening (ECT plateaus below 170°F).
expected_result: ECT should reach 190-210°F within 7 minutes and stabilize. Temperature above 220°F indicates cooling system problem. Temperature that will not rise above 170°F indicates stuck-open thermostat.

title: Coolant Temperature Investigation
instruction: Check coolant level in overflow reservoir and radiator (COLD only). Check for white milky residue on oil fill cap indicating coolant in oil. Check exhaust for white steam which indicates combustion of coolant.
expected_result: Coolant level should be at FULL COLD mark. No milky residue on oil cap. No excessive white steam from exhaust on warm day after warm-up. Any of these signs indicate head gasket concern.

title: Coolant Temperature Investigation
instruction: With engine at operating temperature verify radiator cooling fan operation. For electric fans verify they activate when ECT reaches threshold (typically 212°F). Check fan clutch engagement on mechanical fans.
expected_result: Cooling fans should activate before ECT reaches 220°F. Fan clutch should have significant resistance when spun by hand with engine off and hot. Free-spinning fan clutch indicates failure.
